message:
feat(stdlib/conversion): port strtoi/strtou bounded integer parsers (bd-illc9)

Adds the NetBSD libc bounded integer parsing primitives:

- intmax_t strtoi(const char *nptr, char **endptr, int base,
                  intmax_t lo, intmax_t hi, int *rstatus);
- uintmax_t strtou(const char *nptr, char **endptr, int base,
                   uintmax_t lo, uintmax_t hi, int *rstatus);

Both parse like strtoimax/strtoumax but additionally validate the
result against the inclusive `[lo, hi]` range. On out-of-range or
underlying overflow the returned value is clamped to the violated
bound. `*rstatus` (when non-NULL) receives one of:

- 0      — value parsed and in range
- EINVAL — no digits could be consumed
- ENOTSUP — base outside `0` or `2..=36`
- ERANGE — value out of `[lo, hi]` (clamped)

Implementation:

- core: BoundedStatus enum + strtoi_impl/strtou_impl that wrap
  the existing strtoimax_impl/strtoumax_impl with bounds
  validation. Underlying overflow / underflow maps to OutOfRange.
- abi: thin shims that translate BoundedStatus → libc errno
  constants and write *endptr / *rstatus per NetBSD convention.

Tests: 17 abi integration tests covering: in-range success,
clamp+ERANGE on below-lo / above-hi, EINVAL on no digits with
endptr == nptr, ENOTSUP on invalid base, hex-prefix detection
under base=0, negative values in negative ranges, NULL nptr
returns 0+EINVAL, NULL rstatus is tolerated, partial consumption
records correct endptr, plus the matching strtou variants.
Also bundles a small fmt cleanup to efun_abi_test.rs from the
prior cycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-6azoc","title":"syscall: timer_settime deleted timer handle with null new_value should still return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for timer_settime(timer_id, 0, NULL, NULL) after timer_delete(timer_id) succeeds, so the deleted-handle contract dominates the null-new-value path. -{"id":"bd-6i7pl","title":"PORT: efun(3) family (esetfunc + ealloc/edup/efopen wrappers)","description":"Port the NetBSD efun(3) family of "exit-on-failure" wrappers: esetfunc, estrlcpy, estrlcat, estrdup, estrndup, ecalloc, emalloc, erealloc, efopen. Each wraps the corresponding plain libc function and, on failure, invokes a globally-installed error callback (default: write a brief diagnostic to stderr + exit(EXIT_FAILURE)). esetfunc(efunc) swaps the global callback and returns the previous one. The callback type matches NetBSDs `void (*)(int, const char *, ...)` but is invoked with a pre-formatted message (no varargs from Rust). Tests: success passthrough mirrors plain libc, failure invokes installed callback rather than exiting, esetfunc returns prior callback, NULL src/dst guards.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:14:56.031142956Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T08:14:56.031142956Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-6kwnr","title":"[HIGH] pthread_cond_init rejects a host-initialized pthread_condattr_t with EINVAL (2 tests deterministically fail)","description":"## Symptom

`cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_cond_core_test` has two deterministic failures:

```
test condvar_init_accepts_initialized_attr ... FAILED
test condvar_init_accepts_monotonic_attr_clock ... FAILED

thread 'condvar_init_accepts_initialized_attr' panicked at crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_cond_core_test.rs:86:9:
assertion `left == right` failed
  left: 22
 right: 0

thread 'condvar_init_accepts_monotonic_attr_clock' panicked at crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_cond_core_test.rs:107:9:
assertion `left == right` failed
  left: 22
 right: 0

test result: FAILED. 16 passed; 2 failed
```

rc=22 is EINVAL. Our `pthread_cond_init` is rejecting the `pthread_condattr_t` pointer with EINVAL where the test expects success (rc=0).

## Test bodies (authoritative — not me)

crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_cond_core_test.rs:80 (condvar_init_accepts_initialized_attr):
```rust
let mut attr: libc::pthread_condattr_t = unsafe { std::mem::zeroed() };
assert_eq!(libc::pthread_condattr_init(&mut attr), 0);          // HOST libc init
assert_eq!(pthread_cond_init(cond, &attr), 0);                  // OURS — FAILS here, got 22
assert_eq!(pthread_cond_destroy(cond), 0);
assert_eq!(libc::pthread_condattr_destroy(&mut attr), 0);
```

crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_cond_core_test.rs:96 (condvar_init_accepts_monotonic_attr_clock): same pattern, plus libc::pthread_condattr_setclock(&mut attr, CLOCK_MONOTONIC).

## Diagnosis

The tests use HOST libc's `pthread_condattr_init` / `pthread_condattr_setclock` to populate `attr`, then pass that `attr` pointer to OUR `pthread_cond_init`. Our impl validates the incoming attr against its own expected layout / sentinel bytes and returns EINVAL because the host-initialized bytes don't match.

This is a cross-impl boundary question rather than a pure regression — the test fixture deliberately mixes host-initialized attrs with our cond_init because that is the real-world LD_PRELOAD pattern: user code links against host libc's condattr_init at compile time but our pthread_cond_init interposes at link time. If our cond_init rejects host-initialized condattrs in production, every real user program breaks under FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened.

## Fix direction

Two options:

1. **Relax validation**: treat any non-null `attr` pointer as acceptable, pull the clock / pshared via portable accessors (`pthread_condattr_getclock` / `getpshared`) instead of inspecting our own layout. This matches glibc's own behavior — glibc never rejects a validly-constructed condattr of either origin.
2. **Pass-through**: when the attr's layout doesn't match our internal format, fall through to host `pthread_cond_init` the same way bd-21ypi (138c07c3) routes non-native pthread_t handles. Consistent with the existing host-handoff pattern.

Option 1 is simpler; option 2 matches the existing architectural pattern for 'didn't originate in us'.

## Provenance

Found by cc-libc FULL REVIEW MODE at HEAD 6c0380b4 br-bd-qhnxc: dispatch host pthread cancellation. The bd-qhnxc fix unblocked pthread_abi_test, which let cargo test --workspace --all-targets get further than before and expose this second pthread-cluster failure. Fix commits previously landing on the condvar path: 2e1d65bc 'test(abi): expand pthread condvar core tests', d7b4a832 'expand pthread condvar and thread lifecycle test coverage' — so the tests are intentional coverage, not accidental.

## Blast radius

Two deterministic failures in crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_cond_core_test.rs. Blocks frankenlibc-abi integration-test suite from passing. Doesn't trip any SIGABRT or memory corruption.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:26:39.292180364Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:34:02Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T19:34:02Z","close_reason":"Accepted host-initialized pthread_condattr_t via host getclock fallback; workspace now advances to glibc_internal_abi_test allocator abort","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","condvar","deterministic","full-review","pthread","test"]}

 {"id":"bd-igo1n","title":"PORT: vis(3) extra-bytes family (svis, snvis, strsvis, strsnvis, strsvisx, strsnvisx)","description":"Port the NetBSD vis(3) extra-bytes family: svis(dst,c,flags,nextc,extra), snvis(dst,dlen,c,flags,nextc,extra), strsvis(dst,src,flags,extra), strsnvis(dst,dlen,src,flags,extra), strsvisx(dst,src,srclen,flags,extra), strsnvisx(dst,dlen,src,srclen,flags,extra). These are siblings of the already-shipped vis/nvis/strvis/strnvis/strvisx/strnvisx that additionally treat any byte present in the NUL-terminated extra string as needing escape, even if it would otherwise be a printable passthrough. Implementation: extend core encode_byte with an extras predicate; add thin abi shims; integration tests for extras-trigger-escape, NULL extras = identity to non-extra variant, bounded overflow guards.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:54:16.397672283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:59:16.178210937Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:59:16.176139095Z","close_reason":"shipped: svis/snvis/strsvis/strsnvis/strsvisx/strsnvisx + encode_byte_with_extra core helper + 17 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ihl3","title":"Conformance: add execute_* handlers for dirent_ops family (3 symbols)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:24:38.056550633Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:27:27.621930699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T01:27:27.619986984Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-ijybx","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] pidfd_spawn uses posix_spawn + pidfd_open — PID-reuse race defeats pidfd guarantees","description":"crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs:5573 pidfd_spawn implements via posix_spawn + pidfd_open(child_pid, 0). Between those two calls the child may exit and the PID be recycled, so pidfd_open resolves to the wrong process. This defeats the entire point of pidfd (the race-free reference-a-process primitive). glibc 2.39's pidfd_spawn uses clone3 with CLONE_PIDFD atomically; our CLONE_PIDFD support already exists (frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/mod.rs:375). Fix direction: re-implement pidfd_spawn/pidfd_spawnp via clone3(CLONE_PIDFD) + exec, OR at minimum block SIGCHLD across the window and check the child's start time on pidfd_open. Reach: any LD_PRELOAD'd app that calls pidfd_spawn on short-lived children (common in container orchestration) may signal the wrong process. Same issue in pidfd_spawnp line 5603.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:50:02.842113919Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T01:00:27.928041413Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T01:00:27.926118812Z","close_reason":"Reworked pidfd_spawn/pidfd_spawnp to use clone3(CLONE_PIDFD) and added pidfd spawn regressions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-illc9","title":"PORT: strtoi/strtou (NetBSD bounded integer parsers)","description":"Port the NetBSD strtoi/strtou bounded integer parsers: intmax_t strtoi(const char *nptr, char **endptr, int base, intmax_t lo, intmax_t hi, int *rstatus) and uintmax_t strtou(...). They parse like strtoimax/strtoumax but additionally validate the result against [lo, hi] inclusive bounds, store one of {0, EINVAL, ENOTSUP, ERANGE} into *rstatus, and clamp the returned value into the requested range when out of bounds. Implementation: pure-safe core helpers strtoi_impl/strtou_impl that consume a byte slice + base + lo + hi and return (value, consumed, status); abi shims wrap them around the existing scan_c_string + endptr writeback machinery.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:27:01.260999463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T08:27:01.260999463Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ily44","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] iconv() TOCTOU use-after-free with concurrent iconv_close()","description":"In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/iconv_abi.rs lines 176-188, the iconv() function validates the descriptor handle via is_known_handle(cd) (HashSet lookup), then dereferences via 'unsafe { &mut *cd.cast::<IconvDescriptor>() }'. There is no synchronization between the lookup and the dereference.

If thread B calls iconv_close(cd) between thread A's is_known_handle check and the &mut * dereference, the descriptor's Box is freed (line 350: 'unsafe { Box::from_raw(cd.cast::<IconvDescriptor>()) }'), and thread A then dereferences freed memory --- use-after-free.

POSIX does not explicitly forbid concurrent iconv()/iconv_close() on the same descriptor, but a robust libc should not crash with UAF. Mitigation options:

1. Hold the handles() Mutex for the duration of iconv() (cheap, but serializes all conversions across descriptors).
2. Replace the HashSet<usize> registry with HashMap<usize, Arc<Mutex<IconvDescriptor>>> so iconv() takes an Arc clone before releasing the registry lock; iconv_close removes the registry entry and drops one Arc reference, but the active iconv() call's Arc keeps the descriptor alive until it returns.
3. Use a generational handle scheme (sequence counter + slot index) to detect stale handles.

Option 2 is recommended: per-descriptor mutex avoids serialization across descriptors, and Arc reference-counting cleanly handles the lifetime issue.

Severity: HIGH — UAF in interposed libc function under multi-threaded use.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T01:52:59.789930171Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T03:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T03:30:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: commit c8003957 (peer agent) refactored iconv registry to Arc<Mutex<IconvDescriptor>>; concurrent close + iconv no longer UAFs. 41/41 iconv tests pass including the new TOCTOU regression.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-imo7","title":"Metamorphic tests for raw syscall signal semantics","description":"The syscall veneer tests exercise signalfd4 and timer sigevent but lack direct MR coverage for sys_kill/sys_tgkill/sys_tkill/sys_rt_sigaction/sys_rt_sigprocmask roundtrip semantics. Add: (1) kill(self, 0) succeeds (POSIX null signal permission check), (2) kill(self, invalid_sig) fails with EINVAL, (3) tgkill(pid, tid, 0) ≡ tkill(tid, 0) parity for self-thread, (4) rt_sigprocmask get-set-restore is invertive. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/tests/syscall_veneer_test.rs. One MR per commit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T18:53:42.606540761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:56:47.761683183Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:56:47.759781481Z","close_reason":"Added 4 metamorphic raw-syscall signal tests across 3 commits: 58cfdf4 (kill self null-signal + out-of-range rejection), f7f1055 (tgkill/tkill parity + tgid mismatch ESRCH), 01975f0 (rt_sigprocmask invertive get/set/restore). Fills veneer coverage gap — previously only signalfd4/timer_create_sigevent tested signal-related syscalls.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-imwv","title":"Conformance: direct execute allocator fixture cases","description":"Add direct frankenlibc_fixture_exec coverage for allocator.json cases in the harness alongside existing isolated harness coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T10:37:10.281604078Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T10:41:22.053364549Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T10:41:22.051373321Z","close_reason":"Added direct execute_fixture_case coverage for allocator fixtures and verified with rch test/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocator","conformance","testing"]}
